tool.name: SOPA Aggregate
tool.description: aggregate transcripts and/or channel intensities per cell

param.label: Agregate the genes (counts) inside each cell?
Agregate the channels (intensities) inside each cell?
Expand radius ratio
param.help: Cells polygons will be expanded by `expand_radius_ratio * mean_radius` for channels averaging **only**. This help better aggregate boundary stainings.

param.label: Cells with less transcript than this integer will be filtered.
Minimum intensity ratio
param.help: Cells whose mean channel intensity is less than `min_intensity_ratio * quantile_90` will be filtered.

param.label: Key in SpatialData object containing cell boundaries
param.help: By default, considers the only one image. It can be useful if another image is added later on

param.label: Column of the transcript dataframe to be used as gene names. (Inferred by default)

tool.help:
**What it does**
SOPA Aggregate quantifies molecular and imaging data within each segmented cell. This is a mandatory step that converts spatial data into a cell-by-gene/channel expression matrix suitable for downstream analysis.
The tool can perform two types of aggregation:
1. **Transcript counting**: Count spatial transcripts inside each cell boundary to create a gene expression matrix (for spatial transcriptomics data like Xenium, MERSCOPE, CosMx)
2. **Channel intensity averaging**: Average fluorescence intensity of each channel inside cell boundaries (for multiplex imaging data like MACSima, PhenoCycler, or fluorescence images)
**Requirements**
- Input must be a segmented SpatialData object (output from SOPA Segmentation) - At least one aggregation option (genes or channels) must be selected
**Outputs**
The tool embeds an AnnData table into the SpatialData object containing: - Observations (rows): Individual cells - Variables (columns): Genes (if aggregating transcripts) and/or channels (if aggregating intensities) - Expression matrix: Transcript counts and/or average intensities per cell
This AnnData table can be extracted and used with Scanpy, Squidpy, or other single-cell analysis tools.
**More Information**
See the `SOPA aggregation documentation <https://prism-oncology.github.io/sopa/tutorials/cli_usage/#aggregation>`_.
